title: Electron security and preload audit
summary: Audit and lock Electron security, preload and platform API surfaces for the Windows M6 product gate.
acceptance: Electron settings are audited and locked to nodeIntegration false, contextIsolation true and sandbox true
Preload surface is minimal and typed
Storage and diagnostic APIs expose only whitelisted operations
No generic fs, shell or arbitrary IPC exposure exists

history.details: Instantiated from reviewed WM-0084 M6 DAG; keep proposed until dependencies are done.
Audited and locked Electron security/preload surface: typed allowlist, runtime e2e bridge audit, docs updated; no new host API, IPC, dependency or release scope; required WM-0091 checks pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified Electron settings remain nodeIntegration=false/contextIsolation=true/sandbox=true; preload surface is typed by preload-contract and runtime-checked by desktop-shell e2e; diagnostics remain absent and storage methods remain placeholder allowlist only; no generic fs/shell/ipcRenderer/arbitrary IPC, process/require, local path/secret/full-save leakage, dependency, installer/signing/updater/store/M7 scope, or save-interoperability overclaim. Required checks pass.
WM-0091 verified by independent reviewer; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality.

objective: Verify Electron security posture and preload API minimality before external-test verdict.
forbiddenPaths: generic fs shell or arbitrary IPC exposure
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: external test diagnostics tempt broad IPC.
Risk: file save/export features expose host paths or secrets.
File ownership is limited to Electron main/preload/platform security docs.
ownerGate: Block on any broad host API exposure
Block on changed security settings that weaken sandboxing
Block on secret, path or full-save leakage
m0M5RegressionCoverage: Renderer remains read-only to simulation authority
Save/export diagnostics cannot mutate authoritative world state
benchmarkImpact: No benchmark baseline impact; security audit evidence only.
reviewRouting: systems-architect completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies security settings and preload surface.
rollbackModel: Revert security/preload changes or leave Windows verdict blocked on Electron security evidence.

sparkEligibility.reason: Electron preload and sandbox security are explicit non-Spark boundaries.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Verified Electron settings remain nodeIntegration=false/contextIsolation=true/sandbox=true; preload surface is typed by preload-contract and runtime-checked by desktop-shell e2e; diagnostics remain absent and storage methods remain placeholder allowlist only; no generic fs/shell/ipcRenderer/arbitrary IPC, process/require, local path/secret/full-save leakage, dependency, installer/signing/updater/store/M7 scope, or save-interoperability overclaim. Required checks pass.

integration.summary: WM-0091 verified by independent reviewer; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality.
